title: EMT RNA-seq dataset, subsampled
author: Gautheret, Daniel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Fastq dataset for practising RNA-seq workflows  

 Fastq files are from a study of epthithelium-mesenchymal transition (EMT) by Yang et al. (2016). For teaching purposes, we took replicates corresponding to Day 0 (uninduced cells) and Day 7 (7 days after induction). Original fastq files were obtained from SRA at http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra?term=SRP066794 (public access). We sampled about 0.5% of the total RNA-seq reads. All retained reads are from a single chromosome (chr18). We mapped all reads against the human genome, filtered reads mapping to chromosome 18, and filtered out 50% of the remaining reads. This represents 0.5% of total reads 

 Reference: 

 Yang Y, Park JW, Bebee TW, Warzecha CC, Guo Y, Shang X, Xing Y, Carstens RP. Determination of a Comprehensive Alternative Splicing Regulatory Network and Combinatorial Regulation by Key Factors during the Epithelial-to-Mesenchymal Transition. Mol Cell Biol. 2016. 36:1704-19 (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=27044866)
